Research Interests

About

I am a BS student in Early Childhood Education at Henan University. My research focuses on early childhood education, child socioemotional development, and nature-based education and development.

My current work centers on psychological resilience, connectedness to nature, parent–child and educational contexts, and longitudinal developmental processes in preschool children. I am also involved in research on environmental exposures, brain mechanisms, and developmental outcomes.

This page highlights selected publications and ongoing projects, with compact figure snapshots showing the core model or conceptual framework of each study.

Education

2023–2027

Henan University

B.S. in Early Childhood Education, College of Eurasian International

GPA: 84.03/100. Selected coursework includes Principles of Education (94/100), Preschool Children’s Developmental Psychology (90/100), Thoughts, Emotions, and Behavior: Development and Pedagogical Support (95/100), and Research Workshop (94/100).

Research Experience

2024–Present

Child Development and Nature-Based Education Research Group, Henan University

Research Assistant

Worked on projects involving mediation analysis, cross-lagged panel modeling, and latent growth modeling. Tasks include literature review, manuscript drafting, data cleaning, longitudinal data analysis, and model comparison using SPSS and Mplus.

2026–Present

Environmental Exposure, Brain, and Human Development Research Group, Beijing Forestry University

Research Assistant

Conducted literature search, screening, coding, and synthesis for projects on environmental exposures, brain mechanisms, and developmental outcomes, including meta-analytic structural equation modeling work.

Practicum & Activities

2026

Henan University Affiliated Kindergarten

Student Teacher Intern

Completed an 8-week practicum covering classroom observation, caregiving, collective teaching, activity organization, daily routines, and reflective documentation in a kindergarten setting.

Social Practice

Community and Cultural Engagement

Participated in social practice programs including work at Kaifeng SOS Children’s Welfare Institution and Kaifeng Song Embroidery Art Museum, and received the Advanced Individual award for outstanding performance.

Publications

Under peer review

The Influence of Parental Involvement on Children’s Psychological Resilience: The Mediating Role of Children’s Connectedness to Nature

Wanwan Lou, Xiaomin Li, Beijia Tan, Wenxuan Li

A structural equation modeling study examining how parental involvement relates to children’s psychological resilience, with connectedness to nature as a potential mediating pathway.

Under peer review

Which Comes First? Longitudinal Evidence for Directional Associations Between Psychological Resilience and Connectedness to Nature in Preschool Children

Xiaomin Li, Wenxuan Li, Beijia Tan

A two-wave longitudinal study using a latent cross-lagged panel model to examine the directional relation between psychological resilience and connectedness to nature in preschool children.

Manuscript in preparation

Environmental Exposures, Brain Mechanisms, and Developmental Outcomes: A Meta-Analytic Structural Equation Modeling Study

Authors to be finalized

A meta-analytic project synthesizing evidence on environmental exposures, neural mechanisms, and developmental outcomes, with a focus on mediation pathways and structural integration.

Manuscript in preparation

Modeling the Growth Trajectory of Nature Connectedness: Longitudinal Links with Resilience and Parent–Child Conflict

Beijia Tan, Wenxuan Li, Xiaomin Li

A three-wave latent growth modeling study investigating how baseline resilience and parent–child conflict are associated with trajectories of nature connectedness and later developmental outcomes.

Under peer review

Background, Mechanisms, and Reflections on Japan’s “Transition Bridge Program” between Early Childhood Education and Primary School

Xiaomin Li, Wenxuan Li, Zhetong Zhang

A policy and comparative education analysis of Japan’s transition support system, highlighting governance structures, teacher collaboration, curriculum continuity, and quality assurance mechanisms.

Under peer review

Governance of Preschool Outdoor Play from a Risk–Benefit Juxtaposition Perspective: An Analysis Based on the Canadian Risk–Benefit Assessment (RBA) Toolkit

Xinkai Sun, Wenxuan Li

A governance-oriented analysis of preschool outdoor play that uses the Canadian RBA toolkit to frame communication, threshold setting, dynamic risk management, and incident review.

Published

Mathematical Resilience in Early Childhood: Conceptual Reframing, Contributing Factors, and Educational Implications

Wenxuan Li

A conceptual paper that reframes mathematical resilience for the preschool years, clarifies its early developmental expression, and outlines implications for assessment and educational support.
